items.title: Comparison of medical-grade and consumer wireless EEG systems
items.ident: Comparison of medical-grade and consumer wireless EEG systems
items.category: DOI / paper landing page | DOI landing page
items.notes: DOI extracted; Extracted from Claude Science markdown
items.context: Ratti et al. consumer vs medical EEG comparison | - **Ratti et al. (2017, *Front. Hum. Neurosci.*, ~195 cites):** compared two *medical-grade* (B-Alert, Enobio) vs two *consumer* (Muse, MindWave) systems on the shared Fp1 electrode. Medical-grade and MindWave Fp1 spectra were similar; **Muse showed a broadband increase in power-spectral density and the highest test-retest variability**, and consumer systems were more artifact-prone from eye blinks and muscle in the frontal region. DOI:10.3389/fnhum.2017.00398

items.title: Connectivity-guided intermittent theta burst stimulation versus standard rTMS for depression
items.ident: Connectivity-guided intermittent theta burst stimulation versus standard rTMS for depression
items.category: DOI / paper landing page | DOI landing page
items.notes: DOI extracted; Extracted from Claude Science markdown
items.context: Morriss et al. Nature Medicine RCT | Morriss et al. (2024, *Nat. Med.*): 5-center, double-blind RCT (N=255; cgiTBS n=128 vs standard F3 rTMS n=127). **No difference on the primary outcome** (GRID-HAMD-17; adjusted mean −0.31, 95% CI −1.87 to 1.24, **p=0.689**). Personalized connectivity targeting did **not** beat standard placement. **Grade: Contested/Failed (for the superiority claim).** A crucial counterweight to single-arm personalization enthusiasm. *doi:10.1038/s41591-023-02764-z*

items.context: often gets missed is that humans remain part of this system. These are not fully autonomous networks. They are mixed systems where humans and agents interact in different configurations. One human can coordinate many agents. One agent can serve many humans. Intelligence emerges from that joint system, not from either side alone.All of this leads to a different scaling law. You do not scale intelligence by increasing parameters alone. You scale it by increasing the number and quality of interactions between agents, and by structuring those interactions so that errors are surfaced and corrected.

items.context: , ELI5, methodology▪️ "AI hugging your brain" to explain Schrödinger bridges and other obscure topics▪️ Interactive demo live at jalammar.github.io✨ The magic? The two-step naming process. Cluster independently first (focused context), then rename globally (spot redundancies). This avoids both context overload and duplicate cluster names.Jay's insight: "AI should extend the human mind" to process information at superhuman scale.This is how LLM pipelines SHOULD work:practical, layered, and solving real cognitive bottlenecks.Props to Jay Alammar for the breakdown 🔥Play with it in the comments! 👇

They thought human conversations would take more work, and felt like they'd gotten the validation they need already. To me, this seems like a psychological version of ultra-processed foods. items.title: t has to change.
items.context: t has to change. I joined the alwellco podcast with Andy Jackson and Lauren Parsons to dig into some of the most pressing questions in the field. TMS has proven efficacy, but the “one-size-fits-all” approach leaves too many patients behind. The next frontier is precision: using brain biomarkers, state dependence, and closed-loop optimization to match the right stimulation parameters to the right person at the right time. If you’re curious about where neuromodulation is headed over the next decade and why the integration of neuroscience and engineering is key to getting there, give it a listen.
